mapping.a_element: three-dimensional optical lattice potential hosting the atoms
mapping.b_element: the lattice on which the classical degrees of freedom interact

mapping.a_element: atoms localized at individual lattice sites in the Mott-insulator phase
mapping.b_element: spatially localized discrete-breather solution on the lattice

mapping.a_element: critical value of the ratio of the two competing energy terms (tunneling vs. on-site interaction) that triggers the transition
mapping.b_element: critical lattice dimension d_c above which breather energies have a positive lower bound

brief_justification: The overlap is only the shared setting (a lattice) and the word 'localization' plus the presence of some 'critical value'; the governing machinery differs decisively. Paper A is a quantum many-body phase transition of a Bose-Einstein condensate driven by the competition of tunneling and on-site interaction energies crossing a critical ratio (collective ground-state coherence vs. number-localized Mott phase), whereas Paper B is classical nonlinear Hamiltonian lattice dynamics concerning the existence and energy lower bounds of time-periodic discrete breathers as a function of spatial dimension — no governing equation, order parameter, or transition mechanism corresponds role-for-role.
